The -ExportCsv parameter exports data with columns: Path, Type, UserName, FullName, FolderRoles. For DirectoryGroup entries, the UserName column contains the FullName value to ensure correct resolution when importing with Add-OrchFolderUser.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all users in the current folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchFolderUser ```
Gets all users directly assigned to the current folder (Shared).

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get users by username with wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchFolderUser ytsuda* ```
Gets all folder users whose username starts with "ytsuda" from the current folder. The -UserName parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get users recursively across all folders ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderUser -Recurse ```
Gets all user assignments from all folders in the Orchestrator instance.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get users including inherited assignments ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchFolderUser -IncludeInherited ```
Gets all users assigned to the current folder, including users inherited from parent folders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get users by type and export to CSV ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderUser -Recurse -Type DirectoryUser -ExportCsv C:\temp\folder-users.csv ```
Gets all directory users from all folders and exports them to a CSV file. The CSV can be used with Add-OrchFolderUser for import to another Orchestrator instance.

maml:description.maml:para: Displays help and documentation information for the UiPathOrch module.
Displays an overview of the UiPathOrch module documentation, including the module installation path, available LLM documentation files, PDF manuals, and essential commands for getting started.
The output includes file listings from the module's Docs directory, showing both text documentation files (for LLM consumption) and PDF manuals (for human reference), along with their file sizes.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Returns a formatted string containing module documentation information, file listings, and quick-start guidance.

maml:alert.maml:para: The documentation files are located in the Docs subdirectory of the module installation path. Text files (.txt) are designed for LLM consumption, while PDF files (.pdf) are for human reference.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Display module help information ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchHelp ```
Displays the UiPathOrch module documentation overview, including the module path, available documentation files, and essential commands.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets jobs from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchJob` cmdlet gets jobs from UiPath Orchestrator. You can retrieve jobs by specifying one or more job IDs using the **JobId** parameter set, or by using filter parameters with the **Filter** parameter set.
You must specify at least one filter parameter to query Orchestrator. If no filter parameters are specified, the cmdlet outputs the cached job contents and displays a warning.
When filtering by robot name, the cmdlet batches requests in groups of 10 due to an API limitation.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded folder processing when targeting multiple folders with `-Recurse`.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Jobs?{filter}&$expand=Robot,Machine,Release&$orderby=CreationTime%20desc
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the job ID or IDs to retrieve. Tab completion suggests job IDs from the local cache.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Filters jobs created after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs created before the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that ended after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that ended before the specified date and time.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Filters jobs by a predefined time range relative to the current time. Valid values are: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.
Specifies that the results should be sorted in ascending order. By default, results are sorted in descending order.
Specifies the field to sort the results by. Valid values are: CreationTime, Release/Name, State, SpecificPriorityValue, StartTime, EndTime, SourceType.
Filters jobs by priority level. Valid values are: Critical, Highest, VeryHigh, High, MediumHigh, Medium, MediumLow, Low, VeryLow, Lowest.
Filters jobs by process type. Wildcard characters are permitted. Valid values are: Undefined, Process, TestAutomationProcess. The default value is `Process`.
Filters jobs by the release (process) name. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests release names from Orchestrator.
Filters jobs that were resumed after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that were resumed before the specified date and time.

Filters jobs by robot name. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests robot names from Orchestrator. When multiple robots are specified, requests are batched in groups of 10 due to an API limitation.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Filters jobs by the source that triggered them. Wildcard characters are permitted. Valid values are: Manual, Schedule, Agent, Queue, Event, Studio, Apps, ApiTrigger.
Filters jobs that started after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that started before the specified date and time.
Filters jobs by execution state. Valid values are: Pending, Running, Stopping, Terminating, Faulted, Successful, Stopped, Suspended, Resumed.
Filters jobs created after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs created before the specified date and time.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Filters jobs that ended after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that ended before the specified date and time.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the job ID or IDs to retrieve. Tab completion suggests job IDs from the local cache.
Filters jobs by a predefined time range relative to the current time. Valid values are: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.
Specifies that the results should be sorted in ascending order. By default, results are sorted in descending order.
Specifies the field to sort the results by. Valid values are: CreationTime, Release/Name, State, SpecificPriorityValue, StartTime, EndTime, SourceType.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Filters jobs by priority level. Valid values are: Critical, Highest, VeryHigh, High, MediumHigh, Medium, MediumLow, Low, VeryLow, Lowest.
Filters jobs by process type. Wildcard characters are permitted. Valid values are: Undefined, Process, TestAutomationProcess. The default value is `Process`.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Filters jobs by the release (process) name. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests release names from Orchestrator.
Filters jobs that were resumed after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that were resumed before the specified date and time.

Filters jobs by robot name. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests robot names from Orchestrator. When multiple robots are specified, requests are batched in groups of 10 due to an API limitation.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Filters jobs by the source that triggered them. Wildcard characters are permitted. Valid values are: Manual, Schedule, Agent, Queue, Event, Studio, Apps, ApiTrigger.
Filters jobs that started after the specified date and time.
Filters jobs that started before the specified date and time.
Filters jobs by execution state. Valid values are: Pending, Running, Stopping, Terminating, Faulted, Successful, Stopped, Suspended, Resumed.
You can pipe job IDs to this cmdlet.
You can pipe a folder path to the **Path** parameter.
You can pipe DateTime values to the time filter parameters.
You can pipe string arrays to filter parameters such as **ReleaseName**, **SourceType**, **State**, **ProcessType**, and **Robot**.
You can pipe values to the **Skip** and **First** parameters.
This cmdlet returns Job objects representing UiPath Orchestrator jobs.

maml:alert.maml:para: If no filter parameters are specified, the cmdlet outputs the contents of the local job cache and writes a warning. You must specify at least one filter parameter to query Orchestrator.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get jobs from the last day ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob -Last Day ```
Gets all jobs created within the last day from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get a specific job by ID ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJob -Id 147426438 -Path 'Orch1:\Shared' ```
Gets the job with the specified ID from the Shared folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get running jobs ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob -State Running ```
Gets all currently running jobs from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Filter by process name and state ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob -ReleaseName Blank* -State Faulted,Successful ```
Gets jobs for processes matching the wildcard pattern `Blank*` that are in the Faulted or Successful state.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get jobs with a time range filter ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob -CreationTimeAfter '2026-03-01' -CreationTimeBefore '2026-03-05' ```
Gets jobs created between March 1 and March 5, 2026.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 6: Get jobs from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJob -Last Week -Path Orch1:\Production ```
Gets jobs created within the last week from the Production folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 7: Get jobs recursively with paging ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJob -Last Month -Path 'Orch1:\Shared' -Recurse -First 50 -Skip 100 ```
Gets jobs created within the last month from the Shared folder and all its subfolders, skipping the first 100 results and returning the next 50.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets execution media recordings associated with jobs from Orchestrator.
The `Get-OrchJobMedia` cmdlet retrieves execution media recordings associated with jobs from UiPath Orchestrator. Execution media includes screen recordings captured during job execution.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded folder processing for improved performance when querying across multiple folders.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/ExecutionMedia
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: ExecutionMedia.View
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
You can pipe the **First** and **Skip** values to this cmdlet.
You can pipe the **Path** values to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet returns ExecutionMedia objects representing recordings associated with jobs.

maml:alert.maml:para: Execution media must be enabled on the Orchestrator and the process must be configured to record execution for media to be available.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all execution media in the current folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobMedia ```
Gets all execution media recordings from the current Orchestrator folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get media with paging ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobMedia -First 10 -Skip 20 ```
Gets 10 execution media records, skipping the first 20 results. This is useful for paging through large result sets.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get media recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobMedia -Recurse ```
Gets all execution media from the current folder and all its subfolders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get media from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJobMedia -Path Orch1:\Production ```
Gets all execution media from the specified Orchestrator folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets job statistics from UiPath Orchestrator.
Gets job count statistics from UiPath Orchestrator. The cmdlet returns CountStats objects that contain aggregated job counts grouped by status (such as Successful, Faulted, Stopped, and Running).
The -Path parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available drives.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetJobsStats
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
You can pipe drive names to this cmdlet via the Path property.
Returns CountStats objects containing aggregated job count statistics grouped by status.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet queries the Orchestrator Stats API to retrieve job statistics. When multiple drives are specified, queries are executed in parallel using the thread pool.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get job statistics for the current drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobStats ```
Gets job statistics from the current Orchestrator drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get job statistics from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJobStats Orch1: ```
Gets job statistics from the Orch1: drive by specifying the drive name as a positional parameter.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get job statistics from multiple drives ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchJobStats Orch1:,Orch2: ```
Gets job statistics from both Orch1: and Orch2: drives. The queries run in parallel across drives.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the library packages from UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets library packages (NuGet packages containing reusable workflows) from UiPath Orchestrator. Libraries are tenant-scoped resources stored in the Orchestrator NuGet feed. By default, libraries from the tenant feed are returned. Use the -HostFeed switch to retrieve libraries from the host feed instead.

The -Id parameter supports tab completion and wildcards. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available library IDs dynamically populated from the target tenant. When no -Id is specified, all libraries are returned.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.
Retrieves libraries from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed. When the Orchestrator is configured with a host feed, this switch allows access to libraries shared across all tenants.
Specifies the Id of the library packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant.
Retrieves libraries from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed. When the Orchestrator is configured with a host feed, this switch allows access to libraries shared across all tenants.
Specifies the Id of the library packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.
You can pipe library IDs to this cmdlet via the Id property.
Returns Library objects with properties including Id, Version, and Path.

maml:alert.maml:para: Libraries are tenant-scoped resources (not folder-scoped). They are NuGet packages containing reusable workflows that can be referenced by automation projects.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all libraries in the current tenant ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibrary ```
Gets all library packages from the tenant feed of the current Orchestrator drive. Returns Library objects ordered alphabetically by Id.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get a specific library by Id ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibrary ABC.DEF.GHI ```
Gets the library package with the Id "ABC.DEF.GHI" from the tenant feed. The -Id parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get libraries using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibrary ABC.* ```
Gets all library packages whose Id starts with "ABC." from the tenant feed. The -Id parameter supports wildcards for flexible filtering.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get libraries from the host feed ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibrary -HostFeed ```
Gets all library packages from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get libraries from multiple Orchestrator instances ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLibrary -Path Orch1:\,Orch2:\ -Id ABC.* ```
Gets libraries matching `ABC.*` from both Orch1 and Orch2 tenants. When -Path uses absolute paths, the command can be run from any location.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the versions of library packages from UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets version information for library packages (NuGet packages containing reusable workflows) from UiPath Orchestrator. While `Get-OrchLibrary` returns one entry per library, this cmdlet returns all published versions of each library. Both -Id and -Version parameters support wildcards, allowing flexible queries such as retrieving all versions of a specific library or finding a particular version across multiple libraries.
By default, versions from the tenant feed are returned. Use the -HostFeed switch to retrieve versions from the host feed instead.

The -Id and -Version parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values dynamically populated from the target tenant. The -Version completer filters based on the currently specified -Id value.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetVersions(packageId='{libraryId}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.

Retrieves library versions from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed. When the Orchestrator is configured with a host feed, this switch allows access to library versions shared across all tenants.
Specifies the Id of the library packages whose versions to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant.
Specifies the version of the library packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests available versions based on the specified -Id.

Retrieves library versions from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed. When the Orchestrator is configured with a host feed, this switch allows access to library versions shared across all tenants.
Specifies the Id of the library packages whose versions to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.
Specifies the version of the library packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests available versions based on the specified -Id.
You can pipe library IDs and versions to this cmdlet via the Id and Version properties.
Returns Library objects with properties including Id, Version, and Path. Each object represents a specific version of a library package.

maml:alert.maml:para: Libraries are tenant-scoped resources (not folder-scoped). They are NuGet packages containing reusable workflows that can be referenced by automation projects.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all versions of all libraries ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion ```
Gets all versions of all library packages from the tenant feed. This may return multiple entries per library, one for each published version.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get all versions of a specific library ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion ABC.DEF.GHI ```
Gets all published versions of the library "ABC.DEF.GHI". The -Id parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get a specific version of a library ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion ABC.DEF.GHI 1.0.* ```
Gets all versions of "ABC.DEF.GHI" that match the pattern `1.0.*` (e.g., 1.0.0, 1.0.1, 1.0.2). Both -Id (position 0) and -Version (position 1) are positional and support wildcards.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get versions from the host feed ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion -HostFeed -Id ABC.* ```
Gets all versions of libraries whose Id starts with "ABC." from the host-level feed instead of the tenant feed.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the license information.

The `Get-OrchLicense` cmdlet retrieves the license information from UiPath Orchestrator. It returns a License object that contains details such as the expiration date, allowed and used license counts by type, subscription plan, licensed features, and whether the license is registered or expired.
The `-Path` parameter supports tab completion for drive names.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicense
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions:
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
You can pipe drive names to the **Path** parameter.
This cmdlet returns a License object containing license details such as expiration date, allowed and used license counts, subscription plan, and licensed features.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is tenant-scoped and returns license summary information including expiration date, allowed/used counts, subscription plan, and licensed features.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get the license information from the current drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicense ```
Gets the license information from the current Orchestrator drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get the license information from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLicense Orch1: ```
Gets the license information from the drive named `Orch1`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get the license information from multiple drives ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLicense Orch1:, Orch2: ```
Gets the license information from the drives named `Orch1` and `Orch2`.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets named user licenses.

The `Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser` cmdlet retrieves named user license information from UiPath Orchestrator. It returns LicenseNamedUser objects containing details such as the user name, last login date, machine count, licensing status, and associated machine names.

You can filter results by robot type using the `-RobotType` parameter. If `-RobotType` is not specified, results for all robot types are returned. The `-RobotType` parameter supports tab completion for available robot type values.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives or robot types.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/LicensesNamedUser/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesNamedUser(robotType='{robotType}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.License or OR.License.Read
Required permissions: License.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the licenses to be retrieved. If not specified, results for all robot types are returned. Tab completion suggests available robot type values.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the licenses to be retrieved. If not specified, results for all robot types are returned. Tab completion suggests available robot type values.
You can pipe robot type names to the **RobotType** parameter, or drive names to the **Path** parameter.
This cmdlet returns LicenseNamedUser objects containing details such as the user name, last login date, machine count, licensing status, and associated machine names.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is tenant-scoped and supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives or robot types.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all named user licenses ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser ```
Gets all named user licenses for all robot types from the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get named user licenses for a specific robot type ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser Attended ```
Gets named user licenses for the `Attended` robot type.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get named user licenses for multiple robot types ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser Attended, Development ```
Gets named user licenses for the `Attended` and `Development` robot types.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get named user licenses from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser -RobotType Unattended -Path Orch1: ```
Gets named user licenses for the `Unattended` robot type from the drive named `Orch1`.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets runtime licenses.

The `Get-OrchLicenseRuntime` cmdlet retrieves runtime license information from UiPath Orchestrator. It returns LicenseRuntime objects containing details such as the machine key, machine name, host machine name, service user name, machine type, runtime count, robot count, executing count, online status, licensing status, and enabled status.

You can filter results by robot type using the `-RobotType` parameter. If `-RobotType` is not specified, results for all robot types are returned. The `-RobotType` parameter supports tab completion for available robot type values.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives or robot types.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/LicensesRuntime/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesRuntime(robotType='{robotType}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.License or OR.License.Read
Required permissions: License.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the licenses to be retrieved. If not specified, results for all robot types are returned. Tab completion suggests available robot type values.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the licenses to be retrieved. If not specified, results for all robot types are returned. Tab completion suggests available robot type values.
You can pipe robot type names to the **RobotType** parameter, or drive names to the **Path** parameter.

This cmdlet returns LicenseRuntime objects containing details such as the machine key, machine name, host machine name, service user name, machine type, runtime count, online status, and enabled status.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is tenant-scoped and supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives or robot types.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all runtime licenses ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime ```
Gets all runtime licenses for all robot types from the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get runtime licenses for a specific robot type ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime Unattended ```
Gets runtime licenses for the `Unattended` robot type.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get runtime licenses for multiple robot types ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime Unattended, NonProduction ```
Gets runtime licenses for the `Unattended` and `NonProduction` robot types.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get runtime licenses from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime -RobotType Unattended -Path Orch1: ```
Gets runtime licenses for the `Unattended` robot type from the drive named `Orch1`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Filter enabled runtime licenses ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: Gets runtime licenses for the `Unattended` robot type and filters to show only the enabled ones.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the licensing usage statistics.

The `Get-OrchLicenseStats` cmdlet retrieves licensing usage statistics from UiPath Orchestrator. It returns LicenseStatsModel objects containing the robot type, usage count, and timestamp for each data point.

The `-Last` parameter specifies the time period for which to retrieve statistics. If not specified, the default period is the last 7 days (equivalent to `Week`). Valid values are: Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years. The `-Last` parameter supports tab completion for these values.
Results are ordered by robot type and then by timestamp.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives.
Primary Endpoint: /api/Stats/GetLicenseStats?tenantId={tenantId}&days={days}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: License.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.

Specifies the most recent period for retrieving statistics. Valid values are: Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years. If not specified, the default period is 7 days (equivalent to `Week`). Tab completion suggests available values.

Specifies the most recent period for retrieving statistics. Valid values are: Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years. If not specified, the default period is 7 days (equivalent to `Week`). Tab completion suggests available values.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
You can pipe a time period string to the **Last** parameter.
You can pipe drive names to the **Path** parameter.
This cmdlet returns LicenseStatsModel objects containing the robot type, usage count, and timestamp for each data point.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is tenant-scoped and supports multi-threaded processing when querying multiple drives.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get license statistics for the default period ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats ```
Gets license statistics for the last 7 days from the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get license statistics for the last month ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats Month ```
Gets license statistics for the last month.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get license statistics for the last year ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats Year ```
Gets license statistics for the last year.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get license statistics from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last Week -Path Orch1: ```
Gets license statistics for the last week from the drive named `Orch1`.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the execution logs of the robots.
The `Get-OrchLog` cmdlet gets robot execution logs from UiPath Orchestrator. You can filter logs by time range, log level, machine, process name, Windows identity, and job key.
You must specify at least one filter parameter to query Orchestrator. If no filter parameters are specified, the cmdlet outputs the cached log contents and displays a warning.

When the `-Level` parameter is not specified, the default level is `Info`, which returns logs at the Info level and above (Info, Warn, Error, Fatal). By default, results are sorted by TimeStamp in descending order.
The `-Machine`, `-ProcessName`, `-Level`, `-Last`, `-JobKey`, and `-OrderBy` parameters support tab completion. The `-WindowsIdentity` parameter supports both tab completion and wildcard characters.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/RobotLogs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Logs.View
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Filters logs by the job key (GUID). Tab completion suggests job keys from the local cache.
Filters logs by a predefined time range relative to the current time. Valid values are: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.

Specifies the minimum log level to retrieve. The specified level acts as a threshold, returning logs at that level and above. Valid values are: Trace (all levels), Info (Info and above, the default), Warn (Warn and above), Error (Error and Fatal), Fatal (Fatal only).
Filters logs by machine name. Tab completion suggests machine names assigned to the target folder.
Specifies that the results should be sorted in ascending order. By default, results are sorted in descending order.
Specifies the field to sort the results by. If not specified, the default sort field is `TimeStamp`. Tab completion suggests available sort fields.
Filters logs by the process (release) name. Tab completion suggests release names from Orchestrator.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Filters logs with timestamps at or after the specified date and time. The value is converted to UTC before filtering.
Filters logs with timestamps before the specified date and time. The value is converted to UTC before filtering.
Filters logs by the Windows identity (user name) of the robot. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests Windows identities from the target folder's user robots.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Filters logs by the job key (GUID). Tab completion suggests job keys from the local cache.
Filters logs by a predefined time range relative to the current time. Valid values are: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.

Specifies the minimum log level to retrieve. The specified level acts as a threshold, returning logs at that level and above. Valid values are: Trace (all levels), Info (Info and above, the default), Warn (Warn and above), Error (Error and Fatal), Fatal (Fatal only).
Filters logs by machine name. Tab completion suggests machine names assigned to the target folder.
Specifies that the results should be sorted in ascending order. By default, results are sorted in descending order.
Specifies the field to sort the results by. If not specified, the default sort field is `TimeStamp`. Tab completion suggests available sort fields.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Filters logs by the process (release) name. Tab completion suggests release names from Orchestrator.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Filters logs with timestamps at or after the specified date and time. The value is converted to UTC before filtering.
Filters logs with timestamps before the specified date and time. The value is converted to UTC before filtering.
Filters logs by the Windows identity (user name) of the robot. Wildcard characters are permitted. Tab completion suggests Windows identities from the target folder's user robots.
You can pipe a folder path to the **Path** parameter, or string values to the **Last**, **Level**, **Machine**, **ProcessName**, **JobKey**, and **OrderBy** parameters.
You can pipe DateTime values to the **TimeStampAfter** and **TimeStampBefore** parameters.
You can pipe string arrays to the **WindowsIdentity** and **Path** parameters.
You can pipe values to the **Skip** and **First** parameters.
This cmdlet returns Log objects representing UiPath Orchestrator robot execution logs.

maml:alert.maml:para:
If no filter parameters are specified, the cmdlet outputs the contents of the local log cache and writes a warning. You must specify at least one filter parameter to query Orchestrator.
When the `-Level` parameter is omitted, it defaults to `Info`, returning logs at the Info level and above.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get logs from the last day ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Day ```
Gets all robot execution logs created within the last day from the current folder with the default level of Info and above.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get error and fatal logs from the last week ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Week -Level Error ```
Gets logs at the Error level and above (Error and Fatal) from the last week.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get logs for a specific process ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Day -ProcessName BlankProcess19 ```
Gets logs from the last day filtered by the process name `BlankProcess19`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get logs for a specific machine ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Day -Machine ROBOT-PC01 ```
Gets logs from the last day filtered by the machine name `ROBOT-PC01`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get logs filtered by Windows identity ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Day -WindowsIdentity 'DOMAIN\user*' ```
Gets logs from the last day for Windows identities matching the wildcard pattern `DOMAIN\user*`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 6: Get logs using a time range ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -TimeStampAfter '2026-03-01' -TimeStampBefore '2026-03-05' ```
Gets logs with timestamps between March 1 and March 5, 2026.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 7: Get logs for a specific job key from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLog -Path 'Orch1:\Shared' -JobKey 'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890' -Last Month ```
Gets logs from the last month for the specified job key from the Shared folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 8: Get logs recursively with paging ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLog -Path 'Orch1:\Shared' -Recurse -Last Week -First 100 -Skip 50 ```
Gets logs from the last week from the Shared folder and all its subfolders, skipping the first 50 results and returning the next 100.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 9: Get logs sorted in ascending order ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchLog -Last Day -OrderBy TimeStamp -OrderAscending ```
Gets logs from the last day sorted by timestamp in ascending order (oldest first).

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the log folder path for an Orchestrator drive.
Gets the file system path of the log folder for the specified Orchestrator drive. If the drive-specific log path cannot be determined, the base log folder path is returned instead.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Specifies the Orchestrator drive to get the log location for. If not specified, the current drive is used.
Specifies the Orchestrator drive to get the log location for. If not specified, the current drive is used.
Returns the file system path of the log folder.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet does not make any API calls. It returns the local file system path where logs are stored for the specified drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get the log location for the current drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLogLocation ```
Gets the log folder path for the current Orchestrator drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get the log location for a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchLogLocation Orch1:\ ```
Gets the log folder path for the Orch1 drive.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machines from UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets machine information from UiPath Orchestrator. Machines are entities that represent the physical or virtual hosts on which UiPath Robots run. Each machine has properties including Name, Type (Template or Standard), Scope (Default, Serverless, or AutomationCloudRobot), runtime slots (Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation), AutomationType, TargetFramework, associated RobotUsers, update policy, maintenance window, and tags.

This cmdlet supports filtering by machine name using wildcards, expanding robot user assignments, and exporting results to CSV files. When -ExpandRobotUser is specified, the cmdlet outputs RobotUser objects associated with each machine instead of the machine objects themselves.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completions are dynamically populated from actual machines in the target Orchestrator instances.
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. The -Path parameter accepts Orch drive paths (e.g., Orch1:\) to target specific Orchestrator instances.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Machines&$expand=UpdateInfo
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Read
Required permissions: Machines.View

Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Supports comma-separated values for multiple drives. Tab completion dynamically suggests available Orch drives.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility. Tab completion suggests all available system encodings (e.g., utf-8, shift_jis, us-ascii).
Expands robot user assignments for each machine, outputting RobotUser objects instead of machine objects. Each RobotUser shows the user/machine association including UserName and RobotId.

Exports machines to the specified CSV file path. The CSV includes columns for Path, Name, Description, Type, Scope, UnattendedSlots, NonProductionSlots, TestAutomationSlots, AutomationType, TargetFramework, RobotUsers, UpdatePolicyType, UpdatePolicyVersion, MaintenanceCron, MaintenanceDuration, MaintenanceEnabled, MaintenanceTimezoneId, and Tags. Requires a filesystem path (not an Orch: drive path).

Specifies the names of machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names from the target Orchestrator instances. This parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility. Tab completion suggests all available system encodings (e.g., utf-8, shift_jis, us-ascii).
Expands robot user assignments for each machine, outputting RobotUser objects instead of machine objects. Each RobotUser shows the user/machine association including UserName and RobotId.

Exports machines to the specified CSV file path. The CSV includes columns for Path, Name, Description, Type, Scope, UnattendedSlots, NonProductionSlots, TestAutomationSlots, AutomationType, TargetFramework, RobotUsers, UpdatePolicyType, UpdatePolicyVersion, MaintenanceCron, MaintenanceDuration, MaintenanceEnabled, MaintenanceTimezoneId, and Tags. Requires a filesystem path (not an Orch: drive path).

Specifies the names of machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names from the target Orchestrator instances. This parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.

Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Supports comma-separated values for multiple drives. Tab completion dynamically suggests available Orch drives.
You can pipe machine names to this cmdlet via the Name property, and Orch drive paths via the Path property.

Returns ExtendedMachine objects with properties including Name, Description, Type, Scope, UnattendedSlots, NonProductionSlots, TestAutomationSlots, AutomationType, TargetFramework, RobotUsers, LicenseKey, UpdatePolicy, MaintenanceWindow, and Tags.
When -ExpandRobotUser is specified, returns RobotUser objects with properties including UserName and RobotId, representing the user/machine assignments for each machine.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. You must be on an Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target Orchestrator instances.
When -ExportCsv is specified, no objects are written to the pipeline; all output goes to the CSV file. The RobotUsers column in the CSV resolves robot IDs to user full names.
Multiple Orchestrator drives are queried in parallel for improved performance.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all machines on the current Orchestrator drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine ```
Gets all machines from the current Orchestrator instance.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get machines by name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine machine1 ```
Gets the machine named "machine1" from the current Orchestrator instance. The -Name parameter is positional (position 0), so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get machines using a wildcard filter ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine machine* ```
Gets all machines whose names start with "machine" from the current Orchestrator instance.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get machines from a specific Orchestrator instance ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchMachine -Path Orch1:\ -Name MachineTemp1 ```
Gets the machine named "MachineTemp1" from the Orch1 instance. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\), the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Expand robot user assignments ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine -ExpandRobotUser ```
Gets all machines and outputs the RobotUser objects associated with each machine, showing user/machine assignments instead of machine objects.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 6: Export machines to CSV ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine -ExportCsv C:\temp\machines.csv ```

Exports all machines to a CSV file. The CSV includes columns for Path, Name, Description, Type, Scope, slot counts, AutomationType, TargetFramework, RobotUsers, update policy, maintenance window, and Tags.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 7: Get machines from multiple Orchestrator instances ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchMachine -Path Orch1:\,Orch2:\ ```
Gets all machines from both Orch1 and Orch2 instances. Multiple paths are processed in parallel for performance.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets client secret IDs and creation times for machines.

Gets client secret information (SecretId, ClientId, ClientSecret, CreationTime) for machines in UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet uses multi-threaded processing to retrieve secrets for multiple machines in parallel.
Machines with a Scope of 'PersonalWorkspace' or 'AutomationCloudRobot' are automatically excluded. Machines without a LicenseKey (ClientId) are also excluded.
The -Name and -SecretId parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
The output can be piped to Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret to delete specific secrets.
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. Use the -Path parameter to specify different Orchestrator drives.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/clientsecrets/{licenseKey}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines
Required permissions: Machines.View
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the names of the machines to query. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names (excluding PersonalWorkspace and AutomationCloudRobot scopes).
Specifies the secret IDs to filter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests available secret IDs for the selected machines.
Specifies the names of the machines to query. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names (excluding PersonalWorkspace and AutomationCloudRobot scopes).
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the secret IDs to filter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests available secret IDs for the selected machines.
You can pipe machine names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
Returns MachineSecretKey objects with properties: Path, Name, ClientId, SecretId, ClientSecret, CreationTime, Description, Type, Scope.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. The -Path parameter specifies Orchestrator drives (not folder paths).
This cmdlet uses multi-threaded processing to retrieve secrets for multiple machines in parallel, improving performance when querying many machines.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all client secrets for all machines ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId ```
Gets client secret information for all machines in the current Orchestrator tenant.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get secrets for specific machines ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId machine* ```
Gets client secret information for machines matching `machine*`.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Delete old client secrets ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId | Where-Object CreationTime -LT 2024-10-01 | Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret ```
Gets all client secrets and pipes those created before October 2024 to Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret for deletion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get secrets from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId -Path Orch1:\ -Name machine1 ```
Gets client secret information for "machine1" from the Orch1 tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machine runtime sessions from Orchestrator folders.
Gets machine runtime session information from UiPath Orchestrator. Machine sessions show the runtime status of machines connected to specific folders, including their availability status.
The -Status parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available status values. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
The cmdlet supports multi-threaded folder processing for improved performance when querying across multiple folders.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimesByFolderId(folderId={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: Machines.View or Jobs.Create
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the status of sessions to retrieve. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available status values.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the status of sessions to retrieve. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available status values.
You can pipe the Status and Path values to this cmdlet.
Returns MachineSessionRuntime objects representing the runtime status of machines connected to folders.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Machine sessions are folder-scoped. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
This cmdlet uses multi-threaded folder processing for improved performance when querying across multiple folders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all machine sessions in the current folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchMachineSession ```
Gets all machine runtime sessions from the current Orchestrator folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get sessions by status ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchMachineSession Available ```
Gets machine sessions with 'Available' status from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get sessions recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineSession -Recurse ```
Gets all machine sessions from the current folder and all its subfolders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get sessions from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchMachineSession -Path Orch1:\Production ```
Gets machine sessions from the Production folder on Orch1.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the process packages.

Gets process packages (NuGet packages containing automation processes) from UiPath Orchestrator folder feeds. Each package represents an automation project published to Orchestrator. The cmdlet returns the latest version summary for each package; use Get-OrchPackageVersion to retrieve all versions.

The -Id parameter supports wildcards and tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available package IDs from the target folders. Multiple IDs can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path and -Recurse parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Processes&feedId={feedId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: (Packages.View - Lists packages in a Tenant Feed) and (FolderPackages.View - Lists packages in a Folder Feed)
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
You can pipe package IDs to this cmdlet via the Id property.
Returns Package objects with properties including Id, Version, Description, and FolderPath.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Packages are folder-scoped entities stored in folder feeds. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
The cmdlet retrieves packages in parallel across multiple folder feeds for efficient operation.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all packages in the current folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackage ```
Gets all process packages from the current folder's package feed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get a specific package by Id ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackage BlankProcess19 ```
Gets the process package with the Id "BlankProcess19" from the current folder. Because -Id is at position 0, the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get packages using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackage Blank* ```
Gets all process packages whose Id starts with "Blank" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get packages recursively from all folders ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPackage -Recurse ```
Gets all process packages from the tenant root and all its subfolders. Results are grouped by folder feed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get packages from a specific folder using -Path ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchPackage -Path Orch1:\Production -Id BlankProcess19 ```
Gets the package "BlankProcess19" from the Production folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the versions of process packages.

Gets all versions of process packages (NuGet packages containing automation processes) from UiPath Orchestrator folder feeds. While Get-OrchPackage returns only the latest version summary for each package, this cmdlet retrieves every published version, making it useful for auditing version history or identifying specific versions for export or removal.

The -Id and -Version parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Id completion lists package IDs from the target folders, and the -Version completion lists versions for the selected packages.
When specifying the -Path and -Recurse parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Processes/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetProcessVersions(processId='{processId}')&feedId={feedId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: (Packages.View - Lists versions of a package in a Tenant Feed) and (FolderPackages.View - Lists versions of a package in a Folder Feed)
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to retrieve versions for. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.
Specifies the version of the process packages to retrieve. Tab completion dynamically suggests versions for the selected package IDs.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to retrieve versions for. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the version of the process packages to retrieve. Tab completion dynamically suggests versions for the selected package IDs.
You can pipe package IDs and versions to this cmdlet via the Id and Version properties.
Returns Package objects for each version, with properties including Id, Version, Description, and FolderPath.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Packages are folder-scoped entities stored in folder feeds. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
This cmdlet first retrieves the list of packages (filtered by -Id), then for each package retrieves all versions (filtered by -Version). This may result in multiple API calls for large package collections.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all versions of all packages ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackageVersion ```
Gets all versions of all process packages in the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get versions of a specific package ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackageVersion BlankProcess19 ```
Gets all versions of the package "BlankProcess19" from the current folder. Because -Id is at position 0, the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get a specific version of a package ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchPackageVersion BlankProcess19 1.0.* ```
Gets all versions of "BlankProcess19" that match the wildcard pattern `1.0.*` (e.g., 1.0.1, 1.0.2, 1.0.3). Both -Id and -Version can be specified positionally.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get versions from a specific folder using -Path ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchPackageVersion -Path Orch1:\Production -Id BlankProcess19 ```
Gets all versions of "BlankProcess19" from the Production folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get versions recursively across all folders ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPackageVersion -Recurse Blank* ```
Gets all versions of packages whose Id starts with "Blank" from all folders in the tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets personal workspaces from UiPath Orchestrator.
Gets personal workspace information from UiPath Orchestrator. Personal workspaces are special folders assigned to individual users for their private automations and resources.
The cmdlet returns PersonalWorkspace objects containing the workspace name, owner name, owner ID, and folder ID. Results are returned sorted by workspace name.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completions are dynamically populated from actual personal workspaces on the target drives. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: Units.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the name of the personal workspaces to retrieve. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Specifies the name of the personal workspaces to retrieve. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
You can pipe drive names to this cmdlet via the Path property, or workspace names via the Name property.
Returns PersonalWorkspace objects containing the workspace Name, OwnerName, OwnerId, and folder Id.

maml:alert.maml:para: When multiple drives are specified, queries are executed in parallel using the thread pool.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all personal workspaces ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace ```
Gets all personal workspaces from the current Orchestrator drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get a specific personal workspace by name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace "ytsuda@gmail.com's workspace" ```
Gets the personal workspace with the specified name.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get personal workspaces using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace *ytsuda* ```
Gets all personal workspaces whose names contain "ytsuda".

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get personal workspaces from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace -Path Orch1: ```
Gets all personal workspaces from the Orch1: drive.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets processes from UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets process information from UiPath Orchestrator folders. A process (Release entity) links a published package (NuGet) to a folder, making the automation available for execution. The PackageId (ProcessKey) identifies the underlying package, while the process Name is the display name within the folder.

By default, this cmdlet returns basic Release properties such as Name, Description, ProcessVersion, IsLatestVersion, and ProcessType. When -ExpandDetails is specified, additional properties are fetched including ProcessSettings, VideoRecordingSettings, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, RetentionBucketId, and EntryPointPath.

The -Name parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available process names dynamically populated from the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Releases/?$expand=Environment,CurrentVersion,ReleaseVersions,EntryPoint
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Processes.View
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility. Tab completion suggests all available system encodings (e.g., utf-8, shift_jis, us-ascii).

Fetches full process details from the server, including ProcessSettings, VideoRecordingSettings, EntryPointPath, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, and RetentionBucketId. Without this switch, only basic Release properties are returned.

Exports processes to the specified CSV file path. The CSV includes comprehensive process information with headers: Path, Name, Id, Version, Description, EntryPoint, InputArguments, SpecificPriorityValue, HiddenForAttendedUser, RemoteControlAccess, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, RetentionBucket, StaleRetentionAction, StaleRetentionPeriod, StaleRetentionBucket, ErrorRecordingEnabled, Quality, Frequency, Duration, AutoStartProcess, AlwaysRunning, A4R_Enabled, A4R_HealingEnabled, VideoRecordingType, QueueItemVideoRecordingType, MaxDurationSeconds, and Tags. BucketId values are resolved to human-readable BucketName. Requires a filesystem path (not an Orch: drive path).
Specifies the names of processes to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests process names from the target folders.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility. Tab completion suggests all available system encodings (e.g., utf-8, shift_jis, us-ascii).
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.

Fetches full process details from the server, including ProcessSettings, VideoRecordingSettings, EntryPointPath, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, and RetentionBucketId. Without this switch, only basic Release properties are returned.

Exports processes to the specified CSV file path. The CSV includes comprehensive process information with headers: Path, Name, Id, Version, Description, EntryPoint, InputArguments, SpecificPriorityValue, HiddenForAttendedUser, RemoteControlAccess, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, RetentionBucket, StaleRetentionAction, StaleRetentionPeriod, StaleRetentionBucket, ErrorRecordingEnabled, Quality, Frequency, Duration, AutoStartProcess, AlwaysRunning, A4R_Enabled, A4R_HealingEnabled, VideoRecordingType, QueueItemVideoRecordingType, MaxDurationSeconds, and Tags. BucketId values are resolved to human-readable BucketName. Requires a filesystem path (not an Orch: drive path).
Specifies the names of processes to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests process names from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
You can pipe process names to this cmdlet via the Name property.

Returns Release objects with properties including Name, Description, ProcessVersion, IsLatestVersion, ProcessType, PackageId, and FolderPath. When -ExpandDetails is specified, additional properties are populated including EntryPointPath, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, RetentionBucketId, ProcessSettings, and VideoRecordingSettings.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Processes are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
A process is a Release entity that links a published package (identified by PackageId/ProcessKey) to a folder. The same package can be linked to multiple folders as separate processes.
The -ExportCsv parameter automatically calls -ExpandDetails internally to populate all detail fields. BucketId values are resolved to human-readable BucketName in the exported CSV.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get all processes in the current folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchProcess ```
Gets all processes from the current folder and returns basic Release information including Name, Description, ProcessVersion, and IsLatestVersion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get processes by name with wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchProcess Blank* ```
Gets all processes whose name starts with "Blank" from the current folder. The -Name parameter is positional (position 0) and supports wildcards.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get a process with expanded details ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchProcess BlankProcess19 -ExpandDetails ```

Gets the process named "BlankProcess19" and fetches full details including ProcessSettings, VideoRecordingSettings, EntryPointPath, RetentionAction, and RetentionPeriod. Without -ExpandDetails, these properties are not populated.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Get processes from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchProcess -Path Orch1:\Production -Name BlankProcess19 ```
Gets the process named "BlankProcess19" from the Production folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Get processes recursively from all folders ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchProcess -Recurse | Format-Table Name, ProcessVersion, IsLatestVersion ```
Gets all processes from all folders recursively and displays them in a table format with selected properties.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 6: Export processes to CSV ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchProcess -ExportCsv c:processes.csv ```

Exports all processes in the current folder to a CSV file. The `c:` prefix writes the file to the current directory of the C: drive, which is useful when the current location is an Orch: drive. The CSV includes columns such as Path, Name, Id, Version, Description, EntryPoint, InputArguments, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod, RetentionBucket, VideoRecordingType, Tags, and more. BucketId values are resolved to human-readable BucketName in the export.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 7: Export processes recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchProcess -Recurse -ExportCsv c:all-processes.csv ```
Exports processes from all folders recursively to a CSV file.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets resource requirements for processes in UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets the resource requirements (queues, assets, etc.) defined in the process packages. This cmdlet inspects the package metadata to identify external resources that the process depends on, and validates whether those resources exist in the target folder.

The output includes the process name, resource type (e.g., Queue, Asset), resource name, an optional comment, the validation result (e.g., Success, Error), and the folder where the process resides. This is useful for verifying that all required resources are properly configured before running a process.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completion is dynamically populated from actual process names in the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Releases/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetPackageResources
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Processes.View
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
