Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the job IDs for which to remove execution media. This parameter is mandatory and does not support wildcards. Tab completion shows job IDs that have media available.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the job IDs for which to remove execution media. This parameter is mandatory and does not support wildcards. Tab completion shows job IDs that have media available.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe the **JobId** values to this cmdlet.
You can pipe the **Path** values to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet does not generate output objects.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet processes each matching item individually, so if one removal fails, remaining items continue to be processed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove media for a specific job ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchJobMedia -JobId 12345 ```
Removes all execution media recordings associated with job ID 12345 from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove media for multiple jobs ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchJobMedia -JobId 12345, 67890, 11111 ```
Removes execution media recordings associated with the specified job IDs from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchJobMedia -JobId 12345 -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually removing any media. Use this to verify which media would be deleted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove media from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchJobMedia -JobId 12345 -Path Orch1:\Shared ```
Removes execution media for job ID 12345 from the specified Orchestrator folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes library packages from UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes library packages (NuGet packages containing reusable workflows) from the UiPath Orchestrator tenant feed. The cmdlet deletes specific versions of libraries. Both -Id and -Version parameters support wildcards, allowing bulk removal of multiple versions or multiple libraries in a single operation.
When -Version is not specified, all versions of the matching libraries are removed. The cmdlet supports -WhatIf and -Confirm for safe operation.

The -Id and -Version parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values dynamically populated from the target tenant. The -Version completer filters based on the currently specified -Id value.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Libraries('{libraryId}:{libraryVersion}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution
Required permissions: Libraries.Delete
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the Id of the library packages to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant. This parameter is mandatory.

Specifies the version of the library packages to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests available versions based on the specified -Id. If not specified, all versions of the matching libraries are removed.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the Id of the library packages to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests library IDs from the target tenant. This parameter is mandatory.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Use tab completion to see available drives.

Specifies the version of the library packages to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests available versions based on the specified -Id. If not specified, all versions of the matching libraries are removed.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe library IDs and versions to this cmdlet via the Id and Version properties.
This cmdlet does not produce pipeline output.

maml:alert.maml:para: This operation is irreversible. Use -WhatIf to preview which library versions would be removed before executing the command. Consider using `Export-OrchLibrary` to back up library packages before deletion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove all versions of a library ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchLibrary MyShared.Utilities ```
Removes all versions of the library "MyShared.Utilities" from the tenant feed. The -Id parameter is positional (position 0) and mandatory. Since -Version is not specified, all versions are removed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove a specific version of a library ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchLibrary MyShared.Utilities 1.0.0 ```
Removes version 1.0.0 of the library "MyShared.Utilities" from the tenant feed. Both -Id (position 0) and -Version (position 1) are positional.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove libraries using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchLibrary MyShared.* -Confirm ```
Removes all versions of all libraries whose Id starts with "MyShared." from the tenant feed. The -Confirm parameter prompts for confirmation before each deletion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview removal with WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchLibrary * 1.0.0-beta* -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen if all pre-release versions matching "1.0.0-beta*" were removed across all libraries, without actually deleting anything.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes machines from UiPath Orchestrator.
Removes machine definitions from UiPath Orchestrator. The -Name parameter supports wildcards to remove multiple machines at once.
The -Name parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available machine names. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. Use the -Path parameter to specify different Orchestrator drives.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Machines({machineId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines
Required permissions: Machines.Delete
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the machines to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names from the target drives.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the machines to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names from the target drives.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe machine names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Machines are tenant-scoped entities. They are not associated with specific folders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a specific machine ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachine m1 ```
Removes the machine named 'm1' from the current Orchestrator tenant.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove machines with wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachine m* ```
Removes all machines whose names start with 'm' from the current Orchestrator tenant.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachine Test* -WhatIf ```
Shows which machines would be removed without actually deleting them.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a machine from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchMachine -Path Orch1:\ -Name pool ```
Removes the machine named 'pool' from the Orch1 tenant. When -Path uses an absolute path, the command can be run from any location.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes client secrets from machines.
Removes client secrets from machines in UiPath Orchestrator. Both -Name and -SecretId are mandatory parameters. Use Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId to find the secret IDs for a machine.
Machines with a Scope of 'PersonalWorkspace' or 'AutomationCloudRobot' are automatically excluded.
The -Name and -SecretId parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
This cmdlet accepts pipeline input from Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId, enabling scenarios like deleting old secrets based on creation time.
Machines are tenant-scoped entities. Use the -Path parameter to specify different Orchestrator drives.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /api/clientsecrets/{secretId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines
Required permissions: Machines.Edit
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the names of the machines from which to remove client secrets. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names (excluding PersonalWorkspace and AutomationCloudRobot scopes).
Specifies the secret IDs to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests available secret IDs for the selected machines.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the names of the machines from which to remove client secrets. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests machine names (excluding PersonalWorkspace and AutomationCloudRobot scopes).
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the secret IDs to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests available secret IDs for the selected machines.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe the Name and SecretId values to this cmdlet. This enables pipeline input from Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId.
This cmdlet does not produce output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Machines are tenant-scoped entities. The -Path parameter specifies Orchestrator drives (not folder paths).

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a specific client secret ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret aiai 12345 ```
Removes the client secret with ID 12345 from the machine named 'aiai'.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove all secrets for a machine ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret aiai * ```
Removes all client secrets from the machine named 'aiai'.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove old secrets via pipeline ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId | Where-Object CreationTime -LT 2024-10-01 | Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret ```
Gets all client secrets, filters those created before October 2024, and removes them via the pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret m* * -WhatIf ```
Shows which client secrets would be removed from all machines matching 'm*' without actually deleting them.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the process packages.

Removes process packages (NuGet packages containing automation processes) from UiPath Orchestrator folder feeds. Individual versions can be removed by specifying -Version, or all versions of a package can be removed at once.

The -Id and -Version parameters support wildcards and tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Id completion lists package IDs from the target folders, and the -Version completion lists versions for the selected packages. The -Path parameter also supports tab completion, listing available folder feed paths.
When specifying the -Path and -Recurse parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Use -WhatIf to preview which package versions would be removed before executing the operation.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Processes('{processId}:{processVersion}')?feedId={feedId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution
Required permissions: (Packages.Delete - Deletes a package in a Tenant Feed) and (FolderPackages.Delete - Deletes a package in a Folder Feed)
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders. Tab completion lists available folder feed paths.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the Id of the process packages to remove. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.

Specifies the version of the process packages to remove. If not specified, all versions of the matching packages are removed. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests versions for the selected package IDs.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the Id of the process packages to remove. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests package IDs from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders. Tab completion lists available folder feed paths.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.

Specifies the version of the process packages to remove. If not specified, all versions of the matching packages are removed. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests versions for the selected package IDs.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe package IDs and versions to this cmdlet via the Id and Version properties.
This cmdlet does not produce pipeline output.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Packages are folder-scoped entities stored in folder feeds. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
Removing a package version that is currently linked to a process may cause errors when the process attempts to run. Ensure that no active processes reference the package version before removal.
The internal package and version caches are automatically invalidated after each successful removal.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove all versions of a specific package ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchPackage MyProcess ```
Removes all versions of the package "MyProcess" from the current folder. Because -Id is at position 0, the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove a specific version of a package ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchPackage MyProcess 1.0.0 ```
Removes only version 1.0.0 of "MyProcess" from the current folder. Both -Id and -Version are specified positionally.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchPackage *Test* -WhatIf ```
Shows which package versions would be removed without executing the operation. Useful for verifying wildcard patterns before deletion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove packages from a specific folder using -Path ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchPackage -Path Orch1:\Shared -Id BlankProcess19 -Version 1.0.* ```
Removes all 1.0.x versions of "BlankProcess19" from the Shared folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes personal workspace folders from UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes personal workspace folders from UiPath Orchestrator. The cmdlet first disables the personal workspace for the user, then deletes the personal workspace folder. At least one of -Name or -OwnerName must be specified.

The -Name and -OwnerName parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The completions are dynamically populated from actual personal workspace data on the target drives. When both parameters are specified, they work as an AND filter -- only workspaces matching both criteria are removed.
After removal, the folder cache and personal workspace cache are cleared automatically.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces, DELETE /odata/Folders({folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders
Required permissions: Units.View, (Units.Delete or SubFolders.Delete - Deletes any folder or only if user has SubFolders.Delete permission on the provided folder)
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the personal workspace folders to remove. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Specifies the owner name of the personal workspace folders to remove. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the personal workspace folders to remove. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Specifies the owner name of the personal workspace folders to remove. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe workspace names via the Name property, owner names via the OwnerName property, or drive names via the Path property.
This cmdlet does not produce pipeline output. The personal workspace is removed from the Orchestrator.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet performs two operations: (1) disables the personal workspace for the user, and (2) deletes the folder. At least one of -Name or -OwnerName must be specified; if neither is provided, an error is returned.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a personal workspace by name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchPersonalWorkspace "john.doe's workspace" ```
Removes the personal workspace with the specified name from the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove a personal workspace by owner name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchPersonalWorkspace -OwnerName john.doe@example.com ```
Removes the personal workspace owned by the specified user.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove all personal workspaces using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchPersonalWorkspace * -Confirm:$false ```
Removes all personal workspaces from the current drive without confirmation prompts.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview removal with WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchPersonalWorkspace -OwnerName john* -WhatIf ```
Shows which personal workspaces would be removed without actually removing them.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes processes from UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes (deletes) processes from UiPath Orchestrator folders. A process is the association between a package and a folder; removing a process does not delete the underlying package from the Orchestrator feed.
This cmdlet supports ShouldProcess. Use -WhatIf to preview which processes would be removed, or -Confirm to be prompted before each removal.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completion is dynamically populated from actual process names in the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Releases({processId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution
Required permissions: Processes.Delete
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the processes to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests process names from the target folders.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the names of the processes to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests process names from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe process names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce any output.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Processes are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
Removing a process does not delete the underlying NuGet package from the Orchestrator feed. The package remains available for creating new processes. To manage packages, use the package management cmdlets.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a process by name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchProcess BlankProcess19 ```
Removes the process named "BlankProcess19" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove processes using a wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchProcess *Test* ```
Removes all processes matching the wildcard pattern "*Test*" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchProcess *Old* -WhatIf ```
```output What if: Performing the operation "Remove Process" on target "OldProcess [Shared]". ```
Shows which processes would be removed without actually removing them.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a process from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchProcess -Path Orch1:\Shared -Name BlankProcess19 ```
Removes the process named "BlankProcess19" from the Shared folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Remove processes recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchProcess -Recurse -Name *Legacy* -Confirm ```
Removes all processes matching "*Legacy*" from all folders, prompting for confirmation before each removal.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes queue definitions from UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes queue definitions from UiPath Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet deletes the queue definition itself, not the queue items within it. Errors are handled per-queue, so if one queue fails to be removed the cmdlet continues processing the remaining queues.

The -Name parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available queue names dynamically populated from the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/QueueDefinitions({queueId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues
Required permissions: Queues.Delete
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the names of the queues to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests queue names from the target folders. This parameter is mandatory and positional (position 0).
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.

Specifies the names of the queues to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests queue names from the target folders. This parameter is mandatory and positional (position 0).
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe queue names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce output. Queue definitions are removed from the Orchestrator server.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Queues are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.
This cmdlet removes the queue definition, not the queue items. Errors during removal are handled per-queue, allowing the cmdlet to continue processing remaining queues even if one fails.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a specific queue ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchQueue TestQueue2 ```
Removes the queue definition named "TestQueue2" from the current folder. You will be prompted for confirmation before the queue is deleted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Preview removal with WhatIf using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchQueue Test* -WhatIf ```
Displays what would happen if all queues matching "Test*" were removed, without actually deleting them. Useful for verifying wildcard matches before execution.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove queues recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchQueue TestQueue2 -Recurse ```
Removes the queue named "TestQueue2" from the root folder and all subfolders recursively.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a queue from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchQueue -Path Orch1:\Shared -Name TestQueue2 ```
Removes the queue definition named "TestQueue2" from the Shared folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

maml:description.maml:para: Deletes queue items from a queue.

Deletes queue items from a UiPath Orchestrator queue. The cmdlet accumulates all specified item IDs during processing and submits them as a single batch delete request in the end-processing phase, with a batch size of up to 1000 items per API call.

Each queue item requires a RowVersion value for deletion. If the -RowVersion parameter is not specified, the cmdlet first checks the local cache for the RowVersion and, if not found, retrieves it from the Orchestrator API. Progress tracking is displayed during row version resolution and deletion.
If some items in a batch fail to delete, the cmdlet continues processing and returns the failed QueueItem objects as output. Successfully deleted items produce no output.
This cmdlet supports ShouldProcess. Use -WhatIf to preview which queue items would be deleted, or -Confirm to be prompted before each delete operation.

The -Name, -Id, and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completion is dynamically populated from actual queue names, and the -Id completion shows items from the cache with Status not equal to "Deleted".
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Queues/UiPathODataSvr.BulkDeleteQueueItems
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues
Required permissions: Queues.Edit, Transactions.Delete
Specifies the folder containing the queue. If not specified, the current folder is used. Supports wildcards.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the IDs of the queue items to delete. Tab completion dynamically suggests item IDs from the cache with Status not equal to "Deleted". Multiple IDs can be specified as a comma-separated list.
Specifies the name of the queue containing the items to delete. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests queue names from the target folder.

Specifies the RowVersion (concurrency token) of the queue items to delete. If not specified, the cmdlet resolves the RowVersion automatically by checking the local cache first, then querying the Orchestrator API.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the IDs of the queue items to delete. Tab completion dynamically suggests item IDs from the cache with Status not equal to "Deleted". Multiple IDs can be specified as a comma-separated list.
Specifies the name of the queue containing the items to delete. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests queue names from the target folder.
Specifies the folder containing the queue. If not specified, the current folder is used. Supports wildcards.

Specifies the RowVersion (concurrency token) of the queue items to delete. If not specified, the cmdlet resolves the RowVersion automatically by checking the local cache first, then querying the Orchestrator API.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe a queue name to this cmdlet via the Name property.
You can pipe queue item IDs to this cmdlet via the Id property.

Returns QueueItem objects only for items that failed to delete. If all items are deleted successfully, no output is returned. This allows you to pipe failed items to subsequent retry or investigation commands.

maml:alert.maml:para:
The cmdlet uses two-phase processing: item IDs are accumulated during the process phase and submitted as a single batch delete request during the end-processing phase. The batch size is up to 1000 items per API call.
Each queue item requires a RowVersion (concurrency token) for deletion. The cmdlet resolves RowVersion values in the following order: (1) the -RowVersion parameter if specified, (2) the local cache, (3) the Orchestrator API. Progress bars are displayed during row version resolution and deletion.
If some items fail to delete (e.g., due to a stale RowVersion or concurrent modification), the cmdlet outputs the failed QueueItem objects and continues processing the remaining items.
Queue items are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify the target folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove specific queue items ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchQueueItem InvoiceQueue 48291,48292 ```
Deletes the queue items with IDs 48291 and 48292 from the InvoiceQueue in the current folder (Shared).

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove a queue item with an explicit RowVersion ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchQueueItem InvoiceQueue 48291 "AAAAAAAAB9k=" ```
Deletes the queue item with ID 48291 from the InvoiceQueue, using the specified RowVersion value. This avoids the additional API call to look up the RowVersion.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchQueueItem OrderQueue 55012 -WhatIf ```
```output What if: Performing the operation "Remove QueueItem" on target "Queue: 'OrderQueue [Shared]' Id: '55012'". ```
Shows what would happen without actually deleting any items.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove queue items from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchQueueItem -Path Orch1:\Shared -Name TestQueue2 -Id 70345,70346,70347 ```
Deletes the queue items with IDs 70345, 70346, and 70347 from TestQueue2 in the Shared folder on Orch1.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes roles from Orchestrator.
Removes roles from UiPath Orchestrator. Static (built-in) roles cannot be removed and are automatically excluded from the operation.
The -Name parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available role names. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
Roles are tenant-scoped entities. Use the -Path parameter to specify different Orchestrator drives.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Roles({roleId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Roles.Delete
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the roles to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests role names from the target drives. Static (built-in) roles are automatically excluded.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the roles to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests role names from the target drives. Static (built-in) roles are automatically excluded.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe role names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce output.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Roles are tenant-scoped entities. The -Path parameter specifies Orchestrator drives (not folder paths).
Static (built-in) roles cannot be removed and are automatically excluded from wildcard matching.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a role ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRole MyCustomRole ```
Removes the role named 'MyCustomRole' from the current tenant.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove roles using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRole Test* ```
Removes all non-static roles whose names match 'Test*'.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove a role from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchRole -Path Orch1:\ OldRole ```
Removes the role 'OldRole' from the Orch1 tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes roles from folder users in UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes folder-level roles from users assigned to UiPath Orchestrator folders. The target users can be identified by -UserName or -FullName (at least one must be specified). Both parameters support filtering to narrow down which users are affected.

The -Roles parameter is mandatory and specifies which folder roles to remove. Roles that are not currently assigned to a user are silently ignored. Only folder-level roles can be removed; tenant-level roles are excluded from the operation.
The -Type parameter can filter which directory types to target (e.g., DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, DirectoryExternalApplication).

The -UserName, -FullName, -Roles, and -Type parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. Tab completion for -Roles is context-aware and suggests only roles currently assigned to the target users.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.AssignUsers
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders
Required permissions: (Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit - Assigns users to any folder or if the user has SubFolders.Edit permission on all folders provided)
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the display names of the folder users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests full names from the target folders. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.

Specifies the folder roles to remove from the users. This is a mandatory parameter. Tab completion dynamically suggests roles currently assigned to the target users. Only folder-level roles can be removed; tenant-level roles are excluded.
Filters folder users by directory type. Valid values are: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, and DirectoryExternalApplication. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available types.

Specifies the usernames of the folder users from whom the roles will be removed. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target folders. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.

Specifies the display names of the folder users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests full names from the target folders. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.

Specifies the folder roles to remove from the users. This is a mandatory parameter. Tab completion dynamically suggests roles currently assigned to the target users. Only folder-level roles can be removed; tenant-level roles are excluded.
Filters folder users by directory type. Valid values are: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, and DirectoryExternalApplication. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available types.

Specifies the usernames of the folder users from whom the roles will be removed. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target folders. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe usernames, roles, full names, types, and paths to this cmdlet via the UserName, Roles, FullName, Type, and Path properties.
This cmdlet does not produce output.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Folder users are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders. Personal workspace folders are excluded from the operation.
Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified. If neither is provided, the cmdlet writes an error.
The cmdlet works by reassigning the user with the remaining roles (existing roles minus the removed roles). If all roles are removed, the user remains assigned to the folder with no roles.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a role from a specific user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser ytsuda@gmail.com Executor ```
Removes the "Executor" role from the user "ytsuda@gmail.com" in the current folder. The -UserName and -Roles parameters are positional (positions 0 and 1).

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove multiple roles from a user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser ytsuda@gmail.com "Folder Administrator",Executor ```
Removes the "Folder Administrator" and "Executor" roles from the user "ytsuda@gmail.com" in the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove a role from users by full name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser -FullName "Yoshifumi Tsuda" -Roles Executor ```
Removes the "Executor" role from the user whose display name is "Yoshifumi Tsuda" in the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a role from all users recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser -Recurse -UserName '*' -Roles Executor -Type DirectoryUser ```
Removes the "Executor" role from all directory users in all folders. Users who do not have the role are skipped.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Preview role removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser ytsuda@gmail.com Executor -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen without actually removing the role. The output displays the target user and the roles that would be removed.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes tenant-level roles from users in UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes tenant-level roles from users in UiPath Orchestrator. The target users can be identified by -UserName or -FullName (at least one must be specified). Both parameters support wildcards for matching multiple users at once.
The -Roles parameter is mandatory and specifies which tenant roles to remove. Roles that are not currently assigned to a user are silently ignored.
The -Type parameter can filter which directory types to target (e.g., DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, DirectoryExternalApplication).
This is a tenant-scoped cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies Orchestrator drives (not folder paths).

The -UserName, -FullName, -Roles, and -Type parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. Tab completion for -Roles is context-aware and suggests only roles currently assigned to the target users.
Primary Endpoint: PUT /odata/Users({user.Id!.Value})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.Edit or Robots.Create or Robots.Edit or Robots.Delete
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the display names of the users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests full names from the target tenant. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Specifies the tenant roles to remove from the users. This is a mandatory parameter. Tab completion dynamically suggests roles currently assigned to the target users.
Filters users by directory type. Valid values are: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, and DirectoryExternalApplication. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available types.

Specifies the usernames of the users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

Specifies the display names of the users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests full names from the target tenant. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Specifies the target Orchestrator drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the tenant roles to remove from the users. This is a mandatory parameter. Tab completion dynamically suggests roles currently assigned to the target users.
Filters users by directory type. Valid values are: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryRobot, and DirectoryExternalApplication. Supports wildcards. Tab completion suggests available types.

Specifies the usernames of the users from whom the roles will be removed. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant. Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe usernames, roles, full names, types, and paths to this cmdlet via the UserName, Roles, FullName, Type, and Path properties.
Returns updated User objects after the roles have been removed.

maml:alert.maml:para:
This is a tenant-scoped cmdlet. Users and their tenant roles are managed at the tenant level, not at the folder level. The -Path parameter specifies Orchestrator drives (e.g., Orch1:\), not folder paths.
Either -UserName or -FullName must be specified. If neither is provided, the cmdlet writes an error.
The cmdlet updates the user by replacing the entire role list. It retrieves the full user object, removes the specified roles from the roles list, and writes the updated user back via the PUT endpoint.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a role from a specific user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromUser ytsuda@gmail.com Administrator ```
Removes the "Administrator" role from the user "ytsuda@gmail.com" in the current tenant. The -UserName and -Roles parameters are positional (positions 0 and 1).

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove multiple roles from a user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromUser ytsuda@gmail.com Administrator,"Allow to be Automation User" ```
Removes the "Administrator" and "Allow to be Automation User" roles from the user "ytsuda@gmail.com".

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove a role from users by full name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromUser -FullName "Yoshifumi Tsuda" -Roles Administrator ```
Removes the "Administrator" role from the user whose display name is "Yoshifumi Tsuda".

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a role from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromUser -Path Orch1:\ -UserName ytsuda@gmail.com -Roles Administrator ```
Removes the "Administrator" role from the user "ytsuda@gmail.com" on the Orch1 tenant. When -Path uses an absolute path, the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Preview role removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRoleFromUser ytsuda@gmail.com Administrator -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen without actually removing the role. The output displays the roles that would be removed and the target user.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes test case definitions from UiPath Orchestrator.

Removes (deletes) test case definitions from UiPath Orchestrator folders. The cmdlet first retrieves test cases matching the specified -Name pattern from the target folders, then calls the BulkDelete endpoint to remove each matching test case.
This cmdlet supports ShouldProcess. Use -WhatIf to preview which test cases would be removed, or -Confirm to be prompted before each removal.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completion is dynamically populated from actual test case names in the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/TestCaseDefinitions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.BulkDelete
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSets
Required permissions: TestSets.Delete
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the Name of the test cases to be removed.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the Name of the test cases to be removed.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe test case names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce any output.

maml:alert.maml:para:
Test cases are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders. Personal workspaces are excluded from enumeration.
Removing a test case definition does not delete the underlying package from the Orchestrator feed. The package remains available for republishing.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a test case by name ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestCase LoginTest ```
Removes the test case definition named "LoginTest" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove test cases using a wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestCase *Deprecated* ```
Removes all test case definitions matching the wildcard pattern "*Deprecated*" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestCase *Old* -WhatIf ```
```output What if: Performing the operation "Remove TestCase" on target "OldLoginTest [Shared]". ```
Shows which test cases would be removed without actually removing them.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a test case from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchTestCase -Path Orch1:\QA -Name LoginTest ```
Removes the test case definition named "LoginTest" from the QA folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 5: Remove test cases recursively with confirmation ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchTestCase -Recurse -Name *Legacy* -Confirm ```
Removes all test case definitions matching "*Legacy*" from all folders, prompting for confirmation before each removal.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes test data queues from Orchestrator folders.
Removes test data queues from UiPath Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet deletes test data queue definitions and their items from the specified folders. Personal folders are excluded from processing.
The -Name parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available test data queue names. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/TestDataQueues({testDataQueueId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestDataQueues
Required permissions: TestDataQueues.Delete
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the test data queues to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests test data queue names from the target folders.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the names of the test data queues to remove. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests test data queue names from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe test data queue names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce output.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet processes each matching item individually, so if one removal fails, remaining items continue to be processed.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Remove a test data queue ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestDataQueue LoginTestData ```
Removes the test data queue named 'LoginTestData' from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove test data queues using wildcards ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestDataQueue Old* ```
Removes all test data queues whose names match 'Old*' from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Remove test data queues recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchTestDataQueue -Recurse ObsoleteData ```
Removes the test data queue 'ObsoleteData' from the current folder and all its subfolders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview removal with WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestDataQueue * -WhatIf ```
Shows what test data queues would be removed without actually performing the operation.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes test sets from UiPath Orchestrator.
Removes (deletes) test sets from UiPath Orchestrator folders. Removing a test set permanently deletes the test set definition and its association with test cases.
This cmdlet supports ShouldProcess. Use -WhatIf to preview which test sets would be removed, or -Confirm to be prompted before each removal.

The -Name and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values. The -Name completion is dynamically populated from actual test set names in the target folders. Multiple values can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards.
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/TestSets({testSetId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSets
Required permissions: TestSets.Delete
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the names of the test sets to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests test set names from the target folders.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder with no subfolders included. When -Depth is specified, -Recurse is implied.
Specifies the names of the test sets to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple comma-separated values. Tab completion dynamically suggests test set names from the target folders.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values for multiple folders.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
You can pipe test set names to this cmdlet via the Name property.
This cmdlet does not produce any output.

maml:alert.maml:para: Test sets are folder-scoped entities. You must navigate to a folder on the Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target folders.

Removes the test set named "MyTestSet" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Remove test sets using a wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Remove-OrchTestSet *Legacy* ```
Removes all test sets matching the wildcard pattern "*Legacy*" from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview removal with -WhatIf ---------

Shows which test sets would be removed without actually removing them.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Remove a test set from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Remove-OrchTestSet -Path Orch1:\Shared -Name ObsoleteTests ```
Removes the test set named "ObsoleteTests" from the Shared folder. When -Path uses an absolute path (Orch1:\...), the command can be run from any location.
